text.title: mq-reliability Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for mq-reliability as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: DLQ with no alert and no replay runbook — the common "set it and forget it" configuration.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: DLQ messages are production incidents in slow motion. Without an alert, no one knows they are accumulating. Without a runbook, the replay is ad-hoc and risky. Both are required before a queue goes live.

failure-mode.description: Naive while True retry loop without backoff or ceiling on broker hiccup.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: All consumers retry simultaneously with zero delay, flooding the broker with requests at the exact moment it is recovering. This produces a retry storm that extends the outage. Use exponential backoff with jitter and a hard retry ceiling.

failure-mode.description: Adding a required field to a message schema without a version bump and consumer migration.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Old consumers that do not know the new required field fail on every message after the schema change. Deploy consumer first (backward-compatible, ignores new field), then deploy producer that sends the new field, then bump the schema version and make the field required.
